Home › About
Our Story

Why We Started Forward Family Medicine

Both Dr. Bigley and Dr. Lieberman trained in traditional medicine and witnessed firsthand the strain the insurance-driven system places on patients and physicians — rushed appointments, fragmented care, a system that rewards volume over relationship.

They chose a different path.

By building a Direct Primary Care practice, they committed to keeping their patient panel small, their doors open, and their attention fully on the people in front of them. At Forward Family Medicine, cura personalis — the care of the whole person — isn't a slogan. It's how we practice every day.

Our Promise

You will never be rushed. Your doctor will know your name, your history, and your goals. Your care will be built around your life — not a billing code.

Forward Family Medicine is located in Wayne, Pennsylvania — serving patients across the Main Line, Chester County, and greater Philadelphia. We do not accept insurance. We accept patients.

What Sets Us Apart

We Don't Just Treat What's Wrong. We Build What's Right.

Most primary care practices treat symptoms. We treat causes. Dr. Lieberman's dual board certification in Lifestyle Medicine — paired with his background in culinary arts and nutrition science — means every patient at Forward Family Medicine has access to something most practices simply cannot offer: a physician who understands that food, movement, sleep, and stress are medicine.

And unlike competitors who offer lifestyle medicine virtually or as an add-on service, ours is in-person, integrated into every visit, and included in your membership.

Only DPC practice on the Main Line with In-person, board-certified Lifestyle Medicine
Dr. Lieberman holds Dual board certification — Family Medicine + Lifestyle Medicine
Pre-medical training includes Culinary Arts + Nutrition Science degrees
Built a formal curriculum to train Other physicians in Lifestyle Medicine at Abington
Meet the Team

Your Care Team

Dr. David Bigley

DO — Family Medicine | Co-Founder

Doctor of Osteopathic Medicine Family Medicine PCOM Graduate Christiana Care Residency

Dr. David Bigley, DO, embarked on his journey in healthcare with a strong foundation in neuroscience and biochemistry, earning his undergraduate degree from the University of Scranton. Inspired by a holistic approach to medicine, he pursued his medical education at the Philadelphia College of Osteopathic Medicine (PCOM) in Philadelphia, where he embraced the principle of cura personalis — caring for the whole person.

During his residency in Family Medicine at Christiana Care in Delaware, Dr. Bigley honed his skills while understanding the limitations of the current insurance-based primary care system. Motivated to seek alternatives that prioritize patient well-being, he delved into exploring innovative models of care.

Dr. Bigley's philosophy revolves around truly knowing his patients on an individual level, fostering relationships built on trust and understanding. Beyond the clinic, he finds solace in the outdoors — hiking through nature's trails, or nurturing his creative side in the pottery studio or garden.

Education & Training

  • BS — Neuroscience and Biochemistry, University of Scranton
  • DO — Philadelphia College of Osteopathic Medicine (PCOM)
  • Family Medicine Residency — Christiana Care, Delaware

Dr. Philip Lieberman

MD — Family Medicine + Lifestyle Medicine | Co-Founder

Board-Certified: Family Medicine Board-Certified: Lifestyle Medicine Thomas Jefferson University MD U.S. Navy Physician Culinary Arts + Nutrition Science Built LM Residency Curriculum

Most doctors learn about nutrition in a single semester. Dr. Lieberman went to culinary school first. Then nutrition science. Then Jefferson Medical College. Then the Navy. Then he wrote the curriculum that teaches other physicians how to practice lifestyle medicine. The result is a doctor who can sit with you in the exam room, interpret your labs, and then help you figure out exactly what to put on your plate — and how to make it taste good.

Dr. Philip Lieberman's path to medicine is unlike any other physician you've met. Before medical school, he earned an Associate's Degree in Culinary Arts and a Bachelor of Science in Nutrition Science — building a clinical foundation in food, metabolism, and lifestyle that most doctors simply never receive.

He earned his Medical Doctorate from Thomas Jefferson University in Philadelphia and went on to serve as a primary care physician in the U.S. Navy. After his military service, Dr. Lieberman completed residency training at Abington Family Medicine, where he became dual board-certified in both Family Medicine and Lifestyle Medicine.

During residency, he didn't just learn lifestyle medicine — he developed and implemented a comprehensive curriculum that enabled fellow residents to qualify for board certification through the American College of Lifestyle Medicine.

At Forward Family Medicine, Dr. Lieberman brings that rare combination — clinical medicine, evidence-based lifestyle protocols, and the practical culinary knowledge to make nutrition advice actually work in real life. He lives locally with his wife and two sons, and enjoys rock climbing and spending time outdoors.

Education & Training

  • AS — Culinary Arts
  • BS — Nutrition Science
  • MD — Thomas Jefferson University, Philadelphia
  • Family Medicine Residency — Abington Family Medicine
  • Board-Certified: Family Medicine
  • Board-Certified: Lifestyle Medicine (American College of Lifestyle Medicine)
  • U.S. Navy Physician
  • Developed Lifestyle Medicine curriculum for Abington residency program

Ritamarie Smedile

PhD, RN — Clinical Operations & Wellness

PhD in Nursing Licensed RN Maternal & Child Health Integrative Wellness

Ritamarie brings over a decade of experience in maternal-child health, birth, postpartum care, reproductive health, and integrative wellness. She holds a PhD in Nursing — a research-focused doctoral degree that has prepared her for leadership in healthcare, academic teaching, and scientific inquiry. She has held roles in both high-acuity hospital settings and academic institutions.

As a licensed RN, Ritamarie supports patient care both in the office and virtually, helping with clinic flow, patient services, and wellness coordination. Her approach blends clinical excellence with individualized care, offering medical expertise alongside deep respect for each patient's unique journey.

Ritamarie lives in the neighborhood with her husband and baby girl, and is excited to care for the same community she calls home. She looks forward to expanding her offerings to include pregnancy and postpartum support, menstrual cycle health, Reiki sessions, and community-based workshops.

Note: Ritamarie does not see patients independently. She supports clinic operations and patient services.

Ready for a Different Kind of Doctor?

Start with a free, no-obligation meet and greet. Meet the team, see the office, and ask every question you have. No pressure. No commitment.

Forward Family Medicine is a Direct Primary Care practice. Membership is not insurance. We recommend pairing DPC with a high-deductible or health-sharing plan.
